actions.name: ASK_USER_QUESTION
actions.description: Broadcast 1-4 structured questions back to the user. Each question has a short header, a full question string, and optional multi-choice options with descriptions and previews. This is a structured-question broadcast surface — the action returns the question payload as data so a UI layer can render it; the action does NOT block waiting for an answer. UI integration is pending; for now treat the response as a published question, not as an interactive prompt.

parameters.name: questions
parameters.description: Array of 1-4 question objects. Each: { question: string, header: string, options?: Array<{label, description?, preview?}>, multiSelect?: boolean }. If options is empty/undefined, the question is treated as freeform.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Broadcast 1-4 structured questions to the user (UI integration pending; non-blocking).

exampleCalls.user: Use ASK_USER_QUESTION with the provided parameters.

actions.name: ATTACK_NPC
actions.description: Engage a nearby NPC in the SCAPE world in combat by its instance id (taken from the SCAPE_NEARBY provider's npcs list). The game server pathfinds the agent into attack range and starts combat via PlayerManager.attackNpcAsAgent. Use only when an enemy id is known and combat is desired; this is a write action that mutates world state.

parameters.name: npcId
parameters.description: Nearby NPC instance id from the SCAPE_NEARBY provider.

actions.descriptionCompressed: scape:attack-npc by-id (paths-into-range)

exampleCalls.user: Use ATTACK_NPC with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BASH
actions.description: Execute a shell command via /bin/bash -c <command>. Runs synchronously in the session cwd by default. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code. Hard timeout kills the command. Paths under the configured blocklist are off-limits as cwd.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Shell command to run; executed via /bin/bash -c <command>.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Five to ten word humanly-readable summary of the command.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Hard timeout in ms; clamped to [100, 600000]. Default 120000.

parameters.name: cwd
parameters.description: Absolute working directory; must not resolve under a blocked path. Defaults to the session cwd.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Run a shell command synchronously.

exampleCalls.user: Use BASH with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
actions.description: Create a LifeOps-managed website block rule gated on completion of a specific todo, so the named hosts stay blocked until that todo is marked done. Use when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo (for example, 'block x.com until I finish my workout'). When todoName is supplied with no matching active todo, create that todo first; optional unlockDurationMinutes re-locks the same hosts after the todo gate releases. Do not use for fixed-duration blocks ('for 2 hours') or generic focus blocks ('turn on social media blocking'); those belong to OWNER_WEBSITE_BLOCK.

parameters.name: websites
parameters.description: List of website hostnames to block.

parameters.name: todoId
parameters.description: ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.

parameters.name: todoName
parameters.description: Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.

parameters.name: unlockDurationMinutes
parameters.description: Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Optional profile label for the block rule.

actions.descriptionCompressed: block-websites-until-todo-complete: websites + todoId|todoName + optional unlockDurationMinutes

exampleCalls.user: Use BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BROWSER
actions.description: Single BROWSER action — control whichever browser target is registered. Targets are pluggable: `workspace` (electrobun-embedded BrowserView, the default; falls back to a JSDOM web mode when the desktop bridge isn't configured), `bridge` (the user's real Chrome/Safari via the Agent Browser Bridge companion extension), and `computeruse` (a local puppeteer-driven Chromium via plugin-computeruse). The agent uses what is available — the BrowserService picks the active target when none is specified.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Compatibility alias for subaction from older BROWSER_ACTION calls. Prefer subaction in new plans.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Browser action to perform

parameters.name: tabAction
parameters.description: Tab operation when subaction is tab

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Session or tab id to target

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL for open or navigate

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selector for click, type, or wait

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text for type

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Keyboard key for press

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: Scroll distance in pixels

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Command timeout in milliseconds

parameters.name: script
parameters.description: Script for eval

parameters.name: watchMode
parameters.description: Hint that the user is watching; prefers realistic-* subactions for click/fill so the cursor moves visibly and pointer events fire faithfully.

parameters.name: cursorDurationMs
parameters.description: Cursor animation duration (ms) for realistic-* subactions

parameters.name: perCharDelayMs
parameters.description: Per-character delay for realistic-type/realistic-fill (ms)

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: Replace existing input value when filling (vs append) — applies to realistic-fill

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Cursor target X (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Cursor target Y (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

actions.descriptionCompressed: Browser tab/page control only: open/navigate/click/type/screenshot/state. For LifeOps Browser Bridge settings/status use MANAGE_BROWSER_BRIDGE.

exampleCalls.user: Use BROWSER with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BROWSER_AUTOFILL_LOGIN
actions.description: Autofill saved credentials into an open Eliza browser tab for the requested domain. Requires the user to have pre-authorized agent autofill for the domain via Settings -> Vault -> Logins (`creds.<domain>.:autoallow = 1`).

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Registrable hostname to autofill (e.g. `github.com`, no protocol or path).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Specific saved login to use. When omitted, the most recently modified saved login for the domain is selected.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: When true, submit the form after filling. Defaults to false (fill-only).

actions.descriptionCompressed: autofill save credential open Eliza browser tab request domain require user pre-authorize agent autofill domain via Settings - Vault - Logins (cred domain: autoallow 1)

exampleCalls.user: Use BROWSER_AUTOFILL_LOGIN with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CLOUD_AGENT
actions.description: ElizaCloud agent ops router: provision a container, freeze (snapshot+stop) a running agent, resume a frozen agent from snapshot, or check credit balance and runtime estimate.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Which cloud-agent operation to run: 'provision', 'freeze', 'resume', or 'check_credits'.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name. Required for op='provision' and op='resume'.

parameters.name: project_name
parameters.description: Project identifier (lowercase, no spaces). Required for op='provision' and op='resume'.

parameters.name: containerId
parameters.description: Container ID. Required for op='freeze'.

parameters.name: snapshotId
parameters.description: Specific snapshot ID for op='resume' (defaults to latest).

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Optional description for op='provision'.

parameters.name: environment_vars
parameters.description: Additional environment variables for op='provision' or op='resume'.

parameters.name: auto_backup
parameters.description: Enable periodic auto-backup for op='provision' (default: true).

parameters.name: detailed
parameters.description: Include transaction history for op='check_credits'.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true to execute mutating ops ('provision', 'freeze', 'resume') after the preview.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Cloud agent ops: provision, freeze, resume, check credits.

exampleCalls.user: Use CLOUD_AGENT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: COMMAND
actions.description: Slash-command router. Operations: help, status, stop, models, list. Selects the operation from parameters.op or the detected /<command> in the message text.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Slash commands: help, status, stop, models, list.

actions.name: COMPUTER_USE
actions.description:
computer_use:
  purpose: Canonical cross-platform computer-use action for real desktop interaction on macOS, Linux, and Windows when direct computer operation is required.
  guidance: Take a screenshot before acting. After each desktop action, the result includes a screenshot when available. Use this standard plugin action, not a LifeOps wrapper, for Finder/Desktop/native-app/browser/file/terminal workflows on the owner's machine.
  actions: screenshot/click/click_with_modifiers/double_click/right_click/mouse_move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Desktop action to perform.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Target [x, y] pixel coordinate.

parameters.name: startCoordinate
parameters.description: Start [x, y] pixel coordinate for drag.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description: Modifier keys to hold during click_with_modifiers, e.g. ['cmd', 'shift'] or ['ctrl'].

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Single key or combo string depending on action.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: clicks
parameters.description: Number of clicks for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: scrollDirection
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: scrollAmount
parameters.description: Scroll tick count.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Canonical cross-platform desktop control: screenshot/click/modified click/double/right/move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

exampleCalls.user: Use COMPUTER_USE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: EDIT
actions.description: Replace text in an existing file. Default behavior requires `old_string` to match exactly once; pass `replace_all=true` to substitute every occurrence. The file must have been READ in this session, must still match its recorded mtime, and the new content cannot introduce a detected secret pattern.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the file to edit.

parameters.name: old_string
parameters.description: Exact substring to replace.

parameters.name: new_string
parameters.description: Replacement text. Must differ from old_string.

parameters.name: replace_all
parameters.description: If true, replace every occurrence; otherwise require exactly one match.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Replace exact-match text in a file (single match by default; pass replace_all for multiple).

exampleCalls.user: Use EDIT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: ENTER_WORKTREE
actions.description: Create a git worktree for the current repo and switch the session into it. The new worktree path becomes the session cwd and a sandbox root, so subsequent file operations land there until EXIT_WORKTREE pops it. Use to isolate a parallel branch of work without disturbing the main checkout.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional worktree branch/dir name. Defaults to a random auto-* identifier.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Optional absolute worktree directory. Must lie within sandbox roots. Defaults to a per-call directory under the OS temp dir.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: Optional base ref for the new worktree (default HEAD).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Create and switch into a git worktree for parallel work.

exampleCalls.user: Use ENTER_WORKTREE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: EXECUTE_TRADE
actions.description: Execute a BSC token trade (buy or sell). Use this when a user asks to

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Trade direction: "buy" or "sell"

parameters.name: tokenAddress
parameters.description: BSC token contract address (0x-prefixed, 40 hex characters)

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Human-readable trade amount (e.g. "0.5" BNB for buys, or token amount for sells)

parameters.name: slippageBps
parameters.description: Slippage tolerance in basis points (default 300 = 3%)

parameters.name: routeProvider
parameters.description: Route provider preference for the swap: "pancakeswap-v2" or "0x". Defaults to "pancakeswap-v2".

actions.descriptionCompressed: Execute BSC token trade (buy/sell) via PancakeSwap (admin/owner only).

exampleCalls.user: Use EXECUTE_TRADE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: EXIT_WORKTREE
actions.description: Pop the most recent ENTER_WORKTREE: restore the previous session cwd, drop the added sandbox root, and (with cleanup=true) run `git worktree remove --force` to delete the worktree directory.

parameters.name: cleanup
parameters.description: If true, also `git worktree remove --force` the popped worktree directory.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Exit current worktree, restore previous cwd, optionally git worktree remove --force.

exampleCalls.user: Use EXIT_WORKTREE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: GLOB
actions.description: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. '**/*.ts'). Returns up to 100 absolute paths sorted by mtime descending. Excludes VCS, build, and dependency directories. Use this instead of BASH for file discovery.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Glob pattern relative to the search root (e.g. '**/*.ts').

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path of the directory to search. Defaults to the session cwd.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Find files by glob (e.g. '**/*.ts'); returns absolute paths sorted by mtime.

exampleCalls.user: Use GLOB with the provided parameters.

actions.name: GREP
actions.description: Search file contents using ripgrep (a fast regex search). Returns matching files, counts, or line content. Always excludes VCS directories. Use this instead of BASH for content search.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Regex pattern to search for.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path to a file or directory to search. Defaults to the session cwd.

parameters.name: glob
parameters.description: Optional glob filter passed to ripgrep -g (e.g. '*.ts').

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Optional ripgrep file type passed via -t (e.g. 'js', 'py').

parameters.name: output_mode
parameters.description: How to render matches: 'content' returns matching lines, 'files_with_matches' returns file paths, 'count' returns per-file counts. Defaults to 'files_with_matches'.

parameters.name: -A
parameters.description: Lines of context to show after each match (content mode).

parameters.name: -B
parameters.description: Lines of context to show before each match (content mode).

parameters.name: -C
parameters.description: Lines of context around each match (content mode).

parameters.name: case_insensitive
parameters.description: Match case-insensitively (alias of -i).

parameters.name: multiline
parameters.description: Enable multiline matching (the pattern can span newlines).

parameters.name: head_limit
parameters.description: Truncate output to the first N lines. 0 means unlimited. Defaults to CODING_TOOLS_GREP_HEAD_LIMIT or 250.

parameters.name: show_line_numbers
parameters.description: Show 1-based line numbers in content mode.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Ripgrep wrapper: regex search across files, returns matches/counts/files.

exampleCalls.user: Use GREP with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS
actions.description: Report the current website blocker state by combining the live OS-level hosts/SelfControl status (active hosts, end time, permission notes) with LifeOps-managed block rules (id, gateType, websites, and gate target: todo id, ISO deadline, or fixed duration). Toggle either source via includeLiveStatus and includeManagedRules. Use only for website/app blocking status; do not use for inbox blockers, message priority, morning/night briefs, operating pictures, end-of-day reviews, or general executive-assistant triage.

parameters.name: includeLiveStatus
parameters.description: Whether to include the current hosts-file/SelfControl live block state.

parameters.name: includeManagedRules
parameters.description: Whether to include managed LifeOps block rules and gate metadata.

actions.descriptionCompressed: list-website-blocks: live hosts/SelfControl status + managed rules (gateType, target, websites)

exampleCalls.user: Use LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LIST_AGENTS
actions.description: List active task agents together with current task progress so the main agent can keep the user updated while work continues asynchronously.

actions.name: LIST_LINEAR_COMMENTS

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true before queue changes, downloads, or playlist mutations.

actions.name: TASK_CONTROL
actions.description: Apply a current control operation to an agent-orchestrator coordinator task thread while preserving durable thread history. Operations: pause (suspend with optional note), stop (halt and keep history), resume (re-attach a task session with optional follow-up instruction and agentType override), continue (send a follow-up instruction to the existing or a new task session), archive (hide from active lists), reopen (restore from archive). Resolve the target from threadId, sessionId, or free-text search.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Control operation to apply to the task thread.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: Specific task thread id to control.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Task session id to resolve into a thread when needed.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search text used to find the relevant thread.
parameters.descriptionCompressed: Search text for finding relevant thread.

parameters.name: note
parameters.description: Optional reason for pausing or stopping the thread.

parameters.name: instruction
parameters.description: Follow-up instruction for resume or continue operations.

parameters.name: agentType
parameters.description: Optional framework override for a resumed task.

actions.descriptionCompressed: task-control:op=pause|stop|resume|continue|archive|reopen coordinator-task-thread (threadId|sessionId|search; +note|instruction|agentType)

exampleCalls.user: Use TASK_CONTROL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TASK_HISTORY
actions.description: Query the agent-orchestrator coordinator's current task-thread registry as structured summaries (status, latestActivityAt, optional summary) without loading raw transcripts. Pick metric=list (default), count, or detail; narrow with window=active|today|yesterday|last_7_days|last_30_days, statuses, free-text search, includeArchived, and limit. Use for current work, date-range summaries, topic search, task counts, or one thread's detail.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: Query mode: list, count, or detail.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window for the query.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Topic or free-text search string to match task threads.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Optional status filter list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of thread summaries to return.

parameters.name: includeArchived
parameters.description: Whether archived threads should be included.

actions.descriptionCompressed: task-history:metric=list|count|detail + window + statuses + search + limit (no raw transcripts)

exampleCalls.user: Use TASK_HISTORY with the provided parameters.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Proposal kind: "swap" for a BSC swap quote, "transfer" for a token transfer preview.

parameters.description: Source asset symbol (swap only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT"). One of fromSymbol/toSymbol must be BNB on BSC.

parameters.name: toSymbol
parameters.description: Destination asset symbol (swap only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT"). One of fromSymbol/toSymbol must be BNB on BSC.

parameters.name: fromAddress
parameters.description: Source token contract address (swap only — required when the source asset is not BNB).

parameters.name: slippageBps
parameters.description: Slippage tolerance in basis points (swap only, default 300 = 3%).

parameters.name: toAddress
parameters.description: Recipient EVM address (transfer) or destination token contract address (swap, required when destination is not BNB). 0x-prefixed, 40 hex characters.

parameters.name: assetSymbol
parameters.description: Token symbol to transfer (transfer only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT", "USDC").

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Human-readable amount. For swaps the source-asset units (e.g. "0.5"); for transfers the asset units (e.g. "1.5", "100").

actions.descriptionCompressed: Wallet preview ops: swap, transfer.

exampleCalls.user: Use WALLET_PREPARE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WEB_FETCH
actions.description: Fetch a single http(s) URL and return its body as text. HTML responses are stripped of tags and collapsed to plain text. Body is capped at 5MB; text is capped at 50000 chars. Loopback addresses (localhost, 127.0.0.1, 0.0.0.0, ::1, 169.254.*) are blocked by default; set CODING_TOOLS_WEB_FETCH_ALLOW_LOOPBACK=1 to permit them. Use for reading documentation, blog posts, or pasting in a URL the user referenced.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Absolute http:// or https:// URL to fetch.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Optional summary/extraction instruction. Echoed verbatim in the result text; no LLM is run on the body in v1.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Fetch http(s) URL and return body as plain text (HTML stripped, capped).

exampleCalls.user: Use WEB_FETCH with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WRITE
actions.description: Write a file at an absolute path, replacing any existing contents. The file's parent directory is created if missing. Existing files must have been READ in this session first; otherwise the write is rejected to avoid clobbering external edits.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the file to write.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Full new file contents.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Write a file at an absolute path (creates parents; rejects if existing file was not READ first).

exampleCalls.user: Use WRITE with the provided parameters.
